raw_91_c3_honest_disclosure: sorry count is 2 in delivered file. Both intentional placeholders per Spec §4 unit 1 strategy. No hidden sorry, no admit. 5 of 7 theorems carry zero sorry.

AX1_forward_tail is the load-bearing sorry — without it, the iff main theorem forward direction is unproven for n > 30. Honest claim: bounded forward direction PASS for n in [2, 30]; unbounded tail UNPROVEN in W2. NOT thm.AX1_n6_uniqueness PROVED.

n=1 case sorry is the surface of a Spec quantifier corrigendum. Corrected theorem AX1_n6_uniqueness_corrected is sorry-free at n=1 level (n ≥ 2 premise rules it out). Original wording preserved alongside corrected wording so Spec audit trail is visible.

interval_cases tactic dependency (Mathlib.Tactic.IntervalCases) is NOT in W1 audit USE-AS-IS list; minor audit refinement required. If interval_cases is ever deprecated, AX1_forward_bounded_30 needs refactor to manual case enumeration (29 lemmas).

Toolchain revert (rc2 → rc1) was one-line restoration, not feature change. Mathlib pin remained at rc1; project file simply re-aligned. lake exe cache get fetched 8275 oleans. No user authorization sought because action is restoration to known-good state.

lake build ran on production user Mac M2 (mission §safety jetsam concern). 11.7 s wall-clock; cache hit. Cold-cache full mathlib build attempted ONCE (toolchain mismatch) and did NOT complete; recovery via lake exe cache get succeeded. No SIGKILL observed.
W2 nominal window is 2026-05-05 → 2026-05-18; this delivery is on day -7 (early by 1 week, consistent with W1 early-start pattern).

F-W1-1.evidence: sigma 1, Nat.totient, Nat.divisors all elaborate cleanly under #check via lake env lean; AX1.lean compiles in 6.6s

F-W1-2.evidence: two new Spec corrigenda surfaced in W2: (1) n ≥ 1 quantifier should be n ≥ 2; (2) Mathlib.Tactic.IntervalCases not in W1 USE-AS-IS list
